pi_items.source: Docker Jitsi Meet documentation and community forum posts confirm language files are at this path

pi_items.verified_value: welcomepage.join = 'CREATE / JOIN' (verified from actual jitsi-meet main.json on master branch)
pi_items.source: Direct fetch of https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/lang/main.json shows the actual key is 'welcomepage.join' not 'welcomepage.joinMeeting'

pi_items.verified_value: This key does not exist in Jitsi Meet main.json. Similar keys are: 'dialog.enterDisplayName' = 'Enter your name' and 'dialog.displayNameRequired' = "Hi! What's your name?"
pi_items.source: Direct fetch of https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/lang/main.json shows no 'welcomepage.enterDisplayName' key exists. Web search for exact phrase 'welcomepage.enterDisplayName' returns no results in public Jitsi repositories
Task description explicitly requires agent to save screenshot to this path. Environment setup confirms /home/ga directory exists as user home for 'ga' account

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Jitsi Meet application deployed via Docker image jitsi/web:stable-9753. The localization file is located at /usr/share/jitsi-meet/lang/main.json inside the container. However, the metadata keys are incorrect: the actual key for the join button is 'welcomepage.join' (not 'welcomepage.joinMeeting') with value 'CREATE / JOIN', and the key 'welcomepage.enterDisplayName' does not exist in Jitsi Meet's localization file - the similar key 'dialog.enterDisplayName' has value 'Enter your name'. This means the task metadata contains contradictory key names that do not match the actual Jitsi Meet localization structure.
